seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in BeeAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in BeeAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In BeeAI, sensitive data leakage in AI agents often occurs due to unbounded agent memory accumulation during long sessions, where PII from user inputs persists indefinitely and gets exposed in logs or shared state. Tool validation gaps allow malicious inputs to bypass sanitization and leak data through injected payloads in tool calls. Serialization errors during agent state persistence can inadvertently include raw sensitive data in stored artifacts, while Granite model output inconsistencies lead to unfiltered responses containing leaked credentials or personal information.

howItManifests.scenario: Unbounded agent memory in long conversations
howItManifests.symptom: Conversation history grows without PII redaction, exposing emails/phone numbers in debug logs
howItManifests.impact: All team members viewing agent logs see customer PII; memory bloat causes OOM failures

howItManifests.symptom: User inputs 'email:admin@company.com' in search tool bypassed by weak regex validation
howItManifests.impact: Sensitive credentials returned in tool responses and persisted in agent memory

howItManifests.symptom: AgentState.to_json() includes raw 'user_profile' field with unredacted SSN data
howItManifests.impact: Persisted state files in /tmp/agent_states contain PCI data accessible to all processes

howItManifests.symptom: Model returns unparsed JSON with embedded API keys instead of structured tool_calls
howItManifests.impact: Raw model output with secrets logged to stdout before parsing/validation

detection.name: Memory PII Scanner
detection.description: Scans BeeAI AgentMemory contents for common PII patterns using regex across conversation history

detection.name: Tool Input Validator
detection.description: Intercepts BeeAITool calls and detects sensitive data in parameters before execution
detection.code:
from beeai.tools import ToolCallEvent

def validate_tool_inputs(event: ToolCallEvent) -> bool:
    sensitive_keywords = {'password', 'token', 'secret', 'key', 'ssn', 'credit_card'}
    params_str = str(event.parameters)
    
    # Check for keywords
    keyword_hits = sensitive_keywords.intersection(
        word.lower() for word in params_str.split()
    )
    
    # Check for base64 encoded data (common for secrets)
    base64_pattern = r'[A-Za-z0-9+/]{20,}={0,2}'
    encoded_hits = re.findall(base64_pattern, params_str)
    
    return len(keyword_hits) == 0 and len(encoded_hits) == 0

detection.name: State Serialization Auditor
detection.description: Inspects BeeAI AgentState before JSON serialization for sensitive fields

fixes.name: Safe State Serializer

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with BeeAI through its event listener system, automatically registering detectors for memory growth, tool calls, state serialization, and model outputs. When PII patterns are detected in agent memory or tool parameters, Vex triggers immediate remediation workflows including memory truncation, parameter sanitization, and state redaction. The platform provides real-time dashboards showing leakage risk scores across all BeeAI agents, with automated PRs for code fixes when static analysis finds vulnerable patterns in tool definitions or memory handlers.

Vex's BeeAI integration captures all agent lifecycle events - from message addition to tool execution and state persistence - running parallel scans against 50+ PII patterns and secret formats. High-confidence leaks trigger circuit breakers that pause agent execution until human review, while low-confidence findings feed into continuous training of custom detection models. This closed-loop system reduces MTTR from days to seconds while maintaining 99.9% agent uptime.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from beeai import Agent
from beeai.events import AgentEventListener

# Install: pip install beeai-framework vex-sdk

class VexBeeAIListener(AgentEventListener):
    def __init__(self):
        self.vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-api-key")
        self.vex.register_framework("beeai", version="1.2.0")
    
    async def on_memory_update(self, memory, messages):
        leaks = scan_agent_memory_pii(memory)  # From detection methods above
        if leaks:
            await self.vex.alert(
                issue_type="pii-leakage",
                severity="HIGH",
                metadata={"leaks_count": len(leaks), "agent_id": memory.agent_id},
                auto_fix="redact_memory"
            )
    
    async def on_tool_call(self, tool_call):
        if not validate_tool_inputs(tool_call):
            await self.vex.block_execution(
                reason="sensitive-data-in-tool-params",
                remediation="sanitize_inputs"
            )
    
    async def on_state_serialize(self, state):
        issues = audit_state_serialization(state)
        if issues['issues_found'] > 0:
            await self.vex.enforce_fix(
                fix_type="state-redaction",
                target="agent_state_serialization"
            )

# Register with BeeAI agent
agent = Agent()
listener = VexBeeAIListener()
agent.register_listener(listener)

print("✅ Vex PII protection active for BeeAI agent")
